title: Lemon Zucchini Noodles
subtitle: with Cashew Cream & Crispy Artichoke Hearts

steps.text: Add cashews and ½ cup hot water to a small bowl, and let nuts soak for at least 10 minutes. Drain artichoke hearts and pat dry with a clean towel.

Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add artichoke hearts and cook until crispy in places, 5 to 8 minutes. Transfer crispy artichoke hearts to a plate and sprinkle with salt.
Trim zucchini and slice lengthwise into ¼ inch thick planks. Stack the planks and cut into thin strips to make noodles.

Zest and halve the lemon, juice one half, and cut the other half into wedges. Peel the garlic. Add soaked cashews and the soaking water, lemon juice, peeled garlic, white miso paste, nutritional yeast, and a pinch of salt and pepper to a blender. Blend on high until cashew cream is smooth.
Return skillet to medium-high heat and add zucchini noodles. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and cook, tossing occasionally, until tender, 1 to 2 minutes.

Divide zucchini noodles between serving bowls. Pour cashew cream over noodles and top with crispy artichoke hearts. Sprinkle with lemon zest, hemp seeds, pumpkin seeds, and as much of the red chile flakes as you'd like. Serve with lemon wedges. Tuck in!

tips: * This ingredient will be split between steps.
TIP: We will use this skillet again.
TIP: You can also use a spiralizer or mandoline to make the zucchini noodles.
